name: Osaka (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the bay and river waters that shaped the historic port city
the gold of the miotsukushi channel marker, the city's emblem

description: A navy-blue field charged at the centre with the golden miotsukushi (channel marker) emblem of the City of Osaka.
history: The miotsukushi mark was adopted as the official emblem of Osaka City in 1894, and the dark-blue flag bearing the gold emblem has been used by the municipal government since then. The marker symbolises Osaka's historic role as Japan's water-borne commercial centre, with the Yodo River and Osaka Bay traditionally requiring extensive channel guidance for shipping.

features.symbolism: the deep waters of Osaka Bay and the Yodo River, on which the city was founded as a port

features.role: Miotsukushi (澪標) — the city emblem of Osaka
features.symbolism: Stylised representation of a miotsukushi, the wooden channel marker traditionally placed in shallow water to guide ships into Osaka's harbours. The emblem dates to 1894 and recalls the city's centuries-long identity as Japan's principal commercial port.
